cross_impact_highlights.description: OpenAI $110B funding + 900M users + Pentagon deal + $3T data center buildout form a reinforcing cluster: AI is transitioning from experimental to critical infrastructure status, while simultaneously splitting along ethical fault lines (Anthropic blacklisted for safety stance).

US-Israel attack on Iran with reported killing of Supreme Leader Khamenei represents the most significant military escalation in decades. Expected oil price spike (5-10%), global equity decline, and potential regional war involving Gulf states. Iranian retaliation already targeting US military bases.

Pentagon designating Anthropic as supply-chain risk for refusing to remove safety guardrails creates unprecedented precedent: government coercing AI companies on safety policy. Combined with critiques of AI hype cycle and questions about AI scientific capability.
China's 87-90% domination of humanoid robot market + India-Brazil rare earth pact signals intensifying technology competition and resource diplomacy reshaping supply chains.
